content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcer- PRACTTcES C onrurssroN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street o Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
January 77,2002
Linda Parks
1742Fisk Court
Thousand Oaks, CA9l362
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-01-146
Dear Ms. Parks
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict of
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").I Because you have not
requested advice with respect to a specific pending governmental decision, we can only
provide you with informal assistance.2
QUESTION
Are you required to recuse yourself from the Local Agency Formation
Commission (LAFCO) vote regarding annexation that includes land owned by a
contributor to a county ballot measure committee of which you are a controlling officer?
CONCLUSION
No, you are not required to recuse yourself from the LAFCO vote under
Government Code section 84308(c). However, under Government Code section
84308(b), you would be prohibited from accepting, receiving, soliciting, or directing a
contribution from a party or participant in the proceeding, or their agents, on behalf of
yourself, any candidate, or any committee while the entitlement proceeding is pending
before LAI'CO and for three months after the final decision is rendered in the proceeding.
1 Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Tifle 2, sections
I 8 I 09- I 8997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not prwide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Regulation 1 83 29(c).)
ffi


File No. I-01-146
Page No. 2
FACTS
You are an elected member of the City Council of Thousand Oaks, and you have
announced your intention to run for election to the board of supervisors on August 30,
2001. You have also been sitting as an appointed alternate member of a LAFCO board
since January 17,2001, and as a fulI member of the LAf'CO board since May 16,2001.
You are one of the controlling officers of Save Open Space & Agricultural Resources,
Inc., a county ballot measure committee, which received contributions in excess of $250
in October of 2000 from Eric Johanson. Mr. Johanson is a landowner whose property is
part of a larger annexation request that you anticipate will come before yo'ur LAFCO
board, although no application has yet been filed. You did not receive any contributions
to your own political campargn from Mr. Johanson.
ANALYSIS
Section 84308 (b) of the Government Code states that
"No officer of an agency shall accept, solicit, or direct a contribution of
more than two hundred fifty dollars ($250) from any party, or his or her
agerrt, or from any participant, or his or her agent, while a proceeding
involving a license, permit, or other entitlement for use is pending before
the agency and for three months following the date a final decision is
rendered in the proceeding if the officer knows or has reason to know that
the participant has a financial interest, as that term is used in Article 1
(commencing with Section 87100) of Chapter 7. This prohibition shall
apply regardless of whether the officer accepts, solicits, or directs the
contribution for himself or herself or on behalf of any other officer, or on
behalf of any candidate for office or on behalf of any committee."
(Emphasis added.)
Accordingly, as an officer of LAFCO, you may not accept, solicit or direct a
contribution of more than two hundred fifty dollars ($250) from any party or participant
in the proceeding, or their agents, on behalf of yourself, any other officer of the ageucy,
or on behalf of any candidate for office or any committee while an entitlement
proceeding is pending before LAFCO and for three months following the date the final
decision is rendered in the proceeding.
Under the facts presented here, the proceeding has not yet begun before your
LAFCO board.
Section 8a308(c) further states
"Prior to rendering any decision in a proceeding involving a license,
permit or other entitlement for use pending before an agency, each officer
of the agency who received a corxribution within the precedingl2 months
in an amount of more than two hundred fifty dollars ($250) from a party
or from any participant shall disclose that fact on the record of the


File No. I-01-146
Page No. 3
proceeding. No officer of any agency shall make,participate in making,
or in any way attempt to use his or her official position to influence the
decision in a proceeding involving a license, permit, or other entitlement
for use pending before the agency ifthe oflicer has willfully or
knowingly received a contribution in an amount of more than two
hundred fifty dollars ($250) within the preceding 12 months from a party
or his or her agerfi, or from any participant, or his or her agent if the
officer knows or has reason to know that the participant has a financial
interest in the decision, as that term is described with respect to public
officials in Article I (commencing with Section 87100) of Chapter 7."
(Emphasis added.)
Regulation 18438.6 provides for the acceptance, receipt, solicitation and direction
of contributions under Government Code section 84308. Regulation 18438.6 states:
"For purposes of Government Code Section 84308
(a) A person 'accepts' or'receives' a contribution only if the contribution
is for that person's own candidacy or own controlled committee.
(b) A person 'makes a contribution' to an officer or candidate only if the
contribution is made for that officer's or candidate's own candidacy or
controlled committee.
(e) A person 'directs' a contribution if he or she acts as the agent of another
person or of a committee other than his or her own controlled committee in
accepting a contribution on behalf of or transmitting a contribution to, such
other person or committee."
You have indicated that Mr. Johanson made a contribution to the county ballot
measure committee of which you were a controlling officer, and that his contribution was
not made to your own political campaign. Therefore, based on subdivisions (a) and (b) of
Regulation 18438.6, Mr. Johanson made a contribution to the county ballot measure
committee. Mr. Johanson did not "make" a contribution to your own committee, and you
did not "accept" or "receive" a contribution from Mr. Johanson. We have advised that the
language in 84308(c), by its express terms, would not apply to contributions that were
directed to other persons. (See Nimr Advice Lelter, No. I-86-320.) Accordingly, you are
not disqualified from making or participating in the decision regarding the entitlement
proceeding before LAFCO under Government Code section 84308(c). (See Hiltachk
Advice Letter, No. 4-92-636.)
However, as indicated above, under Government Code section 84308(b), you
would be prohibited from accepting, receiving, soliciting, or directing a contribution from
a party or participant in the proceeding, or their agents, on behalf of yourself; any


File No. I-01-146
Page No. 4
candidate, or any committee while the entitlement proceeding is pending and for three
months after the final decision is rendered in the proceeding.
If you have any other questions regarding this matteq please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
{
By:
B. Armstrong
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

before LAI'CO and for three months after the final decision is rendered in the proceeding.
2 Informal assistance does not prwide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Regulation 1 83 29(c).)

Section 84308 (b) of the Government Code states that

more than two hundred fifty dollars ($250) from any party, or his or her
agerrt, or from any participant, or his or her agent, while a proceeding

Section 8a308(c) further states

permit or other entitlement for use pending before an agency, each officer

in an amount of more than two hundred fifty dollars ($250) from a party
or from any participant shall disclose that fact on the record of the

or in any way attempt to use his or her official position to influence the

hundred fifty dollars ($250) within the preceding 12 months from a party

However, as indicated above, under Government Code section 84308(b), you

Commission (LAFCO) vote regarding annexation that includes land owned by a
contributor to a county ballot measure committee of which you are a controlling officer?

CONCLUSION: No, you are not required to recuse yourself from the LAFCO vote under
Government Code section 84308(c). However, under Government Code section
84308(b), you would be prohibited from accepting, receiving, soliciting, or directing a
contribution from a party or participant in the proceeding, or their agents, on behalf of
yourself, any candidate, or any committee while the entitlement proceeding is pending
before LAI'CO and for three months after the final decision is rendered in the proceeding.
2 Informal assistance does not prwide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Regulation 1 83 29(c).)
